prompt: Completa: 'Penso che io ____ troppo.' (parlare)
explanation: After an opinion verb like penso che, the verb in the che-clause goes into the present subjunctive. For -are verbs the three singular persons are all the same: io, tu and lui/lei all give parli. Build it from the stem parl- plus -i.
examiner_note: The frame 'che io' supplies the person; cue names only the verb (Rev 9 cue economy). Singular kept load-bearing via the explicit subject (identical-singulars note).

prompt: Completa: 'Credo che Maria ____ a Roma.' (abitare)
explanation: The 3rd-person singular present subjunctive of an -are verb ends in -i: abiti. The indicative abita (with -a) is the form to avoid here; the opinion verb credo che forces the subjunctive abiti.
examiner_note: The indicative 'abita' is the signature wrong form for the -are singular subjunctive; it sits in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Spero che i bambini non ____ durante il film.' (parlare)
explanation: The 3rd-person plural present subjunctive of an -are verb is -ino: parlino. The common slip is the indicative -ano (parlano), which differs by a single vowel; spero che pins the subjunctive.
examiner_note: The -ino vs indicative -ano contrast is the headline -are 3pl trap; parlano in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'È importante che voi ____ con onestà.' (parlare)
explanation: The voi present subjunctive of an -are verb is -iate: parliate, with an i the indicative voi form (parlate) does not have. È importante che is an impersonal trigger that requires the subjunctive.
examiner_note: Subjunctive parliate vs indicative parlate is the voi trap; parlate in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Voglio che tu ____ di più.' (lavorare)
explanation: Voglio che (a verb of will) requires the subjunctive in the che-clause. The tu present subjunctive of an -are verb is -i: lavori. Avoid the -a form (lavora), which is the indicative 3sg and the imperative.
examiner_note: tu subjunctive lavori coincides with the indicative tu; the diagnostic miss here is the -a form, caught by lavora in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Bisogna che loro ci ____ un momento.' (aspettare)
explanation: Bisogna che requires the subjunctive. The -are 3rd-person plural subjunctive is -ino: aspettino, not the indicative aspettano.

prompt: Completa: 'Penso che io ____ il treno delle otto.' (prendere)
explanation: Unlike -are verbs (which take -i), -ere and -ire verbs take -a in all three singular persons of the present subjunctive: prenda. So the io form here is prenda, not the indicative prendo.
examiner_note: The -ere/-ire singular ending is -a; the -i form (prendi, the -are pattern misapplied) is the cross-class miss, in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Credo che Luca ____ molto bene.' (scrivere)
explanation: The 3rd-person singular present subjunctive of an -ere verb is -a: scriva. The indicative scrive (with -e) is the form credo che rules out.
examiner_note: Indicative scrive is the 3sg trap; in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Spero che ____ presto.' (partire, loro)
explanation: The 3rd-person plural present subjunctive of an -ere/-ire verb is -ano: partano. The frequent slip is the indicative -ono (partono); they differ by one vowel. Spero che forces the subjunctive.
examiner_note: No subject in the sentence, so the cue carries the person (partire, loro). The -ano vs indicative -ono contrast is the 3pl trap; partono in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Voglio che tu ____ una decisione.' (prendere)

translation.reveal: prendere - to take / to make (a decision)

explanation: The tu present subjunctive of an -ere verb is -a, the same as the other two singular persons: prenda. The tempting wrong form is prendi (the -i ending borrowed from -are verbs, and also the indicative tu). Voglio che requires the subjunctive.
examiner_note: prendi (the -are ending on an -ere verb, also the indicative tu) is the key cross-class miss; in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'È bene che voi ____ almeno otto ore.' (dormire)
explanation: The voi present subjunctive is -iate for every conjugation: dormiate. The indicative voi form (dormite) is the slip; the impersonal trigger è bene che requires the subjunctive.

prompt: Completa: 'Dubito che il negozio ____ la domenica.' (aprire)
explanation: Aprire is a plain -ire verb (no -isc-), so the 3rd-person singular subjunctive is the stem plus -a: apra. Dubito che (a verb of doubt) requires the subjunctive, ruling out the indicative apre.

prompt: Completa: 'Non credo che io ____ bene questa regola.' (capire)
explanation: Capire belongs to the -isc- group: it inserts -isc- before the ending in the three singular persons. So the subjunctive singular is capisca, built the same way as the indicative capisco but with the subjunctive -a.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed: it names the -isc- class membership, which the -ire infinitive does not reveal (criterion 15 leak case).

prompt: Completa: 'Spero che il film ____ presto.' (finire)
explanation: Finire is an -isc- verb, so the singular subjunctive inserts -isc-: finisca. The indicative finisce is the slip; spero che forces the subjunctive.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed (names -isc- membership). Indicative finisce in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Penso che loro ____ il mare alla montagna.' (preferire)
explanation: The -isc- also appears in the 3rd-person plural: preferiscano. Two things to get right at once here, the -isc- insert and the subjunctive ending -ano (not the indicative -ono of preferiscono).
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Double trap: keep -isc- and use -ano not -ono; preferiscono in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Bisogna che noi ____ il problema prima di decidere.' (capire)
explanation: The -isc- insert appears in the singular and the 3rd-person plural, but never in the noi form. The noi present subjunctive is the plain stem plus -iamo: capiamo, identical to the indicative noi. Carrying the -isc- in (capisciamo) is the over-extension to avoid.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb left visible deliberately: naming the -isc- class here tempts the catchable over-extension capisciamo, which is in must_not_include (criterion 15 productive-trap case).

prompt: Completa: 'È importante che voi ____ il lavoro oggi.' (finire)
explanation: Like the noi form, the voi present subjunctive of an -isc- verb has no -isc-: finiate. The two over-extensions to avoid are finisciate (keeping the insert) and finite (the plain indicative voi).
examiner_note: Breadcrumb left visible: tempts finisciate, the catchable over-extension, in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Voglio che tu ____ una soluzione.' (cercare)

translation.reveal: cercare - to look for / to search

explanation: Because the -are subjunctive ending starts with -i, a verb in -care inserts an h to keep the hard c sound: cercare gives cerchi. Without the h you would read a soft c (cerci), which is wrong.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed: it names the rule-output 'add h', which is the skill under test (criterion 15).

prompt: Completa: 'È giusto che ____ il conto loro.' (pagare, loro)
explanation: Verbs in -gare insert an h before the -i of the subjunctive to keep the hard g: pagare gives paghino in the 3rd-person plural. Two things to land: the h (not pagino) and the -ino ending (not the indicative pagano).
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed (names 'add h'). pagino misses the h; pagano is the indicative; both in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Spero che la lezione ____ in orario.' (cominciare)
explanation: Verbs in -ciare already carry an i that only softens the c, so when the subjunctive ending -i is added the two merge into one: cominciare gives cominci, not comincii. The indicative comincia is the other form to avoid.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed (names the drop-i rule). comincii keeps the redundant i; comincia is the indicative.

prompt: Completa: 'Bisogna che i bambini ____ più verdura.' (mangiare)
explanation: Verbs in -giare drop the soft-g i before the subjunctive ending: mangiare gives mangino in the 3rd-person plural, not mangiino. The indicative mangiano is the other slip.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed (drop-i rule-output). mangiino keeps the i; mangiano is indicative.

prompt: Completa: 'Spero che Marco ____ alla festa stasera.' (venire)
explanation: For this large irregular group, build the present subjunctive from the io form of the present indicative: take vengo, drop the -o, add the ending. That gives venga for all three singular persons. Building from the infinitive (vena) misses the -g-.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed: it names a class membership (from-1sg irregular) not derivable from the infinitive. Indicative viene in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Penso che ____ tutti alla riunione domani.' (venire, loro)
explanation: The 3rd-person plural keeps the same -g- stem from vengo and takes -ano: vengano. The signature miss is the indicative vengono, which differs only in the final vowels.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative 3pl vengono is the headline from-1sg trap; in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Voglio che tu ____ attenzione.' (fare)
explanation: From the io form faccio, drop the -o and add the ending: faccia, for all three singular persons. The double c comes straight from faccio; the infinitive fare would mislead you toward fa or fai.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative fai in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'È importante che i ragazzi ____ i compiti.' (fare)
explanation: The 3rd-person plural keeps the faccio stem and adds -ano: facciano. The indicative fanno is the slip to avoid.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative 3pl fanno in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Bisogna che lui ____ dal medico.' (andare)
explanation: From the io form vado, drop the -o and add -a: vada. The infinitive andare would mislead toward a regular *andi; the io form is the safe base.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative va in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Credo che ____ via presto.' (andare, loro)
explanation: The 3rd-person plural keeps the vado stem and adds -ano: vadano. The indicative vanno is the form to avoid.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative 3pl vanno in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Non credo che lei ____ farlo da sola.' (potere)
explanation: From the io form posso, drop the -o and add -a: possa, with the double s carried over from posso. The indicative può is the slip.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative può in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Penso che Anna ____ restare ancora un po'.' (volere)
explanation: From the io form voglio, drop the -o and add -a: voglia, keeping the -gli- of voglio. The indicative vuole, with its diphthong, is the form to avoid.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative vuole in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Dubito che loro ____ la verità.' (dire)
explanation: From the io form dico comes the dic- stem: the 3rd-person plural subjunctive is dicano. The indicative dicono is the slip; note the c stays hard before -ano with no need for an h.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative 3pl dicono in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'È meglio che lei ____ presto di casa.' (uscire)

translation.reveal: uscire - to go out / to leave

explanation: Uscire shifts u to e in the stressed forms, visible in the io indicative esco. Build from there: esca. The infinitive would mislead toward usca; the indicative esce is the other slip.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative esce in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Voglio che tu ____ la porta chiusa.' (tenere)
explanation: Tenere has the -g- form tengo in the io present, so the subjunctive is tenga. The indicative tieni (with the diphthong) is the slip to avoid.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative tieni in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Mi dispiace che lui ____ partire così presto.' (dovere)

any_phrases.note: standard but less common variant; debba is the more usual form

explanation: Dovere has two standard singular subjunctive forms: debba (the more common) and deva. Both are correct; debba is preferred in careful writing. The indicative deve is the slip to avoid here, ruled out by the emotion trigger mi dispiace che.
examiner_note: debba full credit, deva graded 0.9. Flagged for the project author in coverage: confirm the credit split is house policy.

prompt: Completa: 'Penso che ____ una buona idea.' (essere)
explanation: Essere has its own subjunctive that cannot be derived from the indicative: sia for all three singular persons (and siamo, siate, siano). This is the single most useful form in the whole tree because sia is also the auxiliary for the compound past subjunctive. The indicative è is the slip.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed: suppletive forms are not derivable from the infinitive or the 1sg. Indicative è and bare e in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Sembra che io ____ sempre in ritardo.' (essere)
explanation: The three singular persons of essere's subjunctive are identical: io, tu and lui/lei all give sia. Here the io form is sia, not the indicative sono. Sembra che is an impersonal trigger.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative 1sg sono in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Spero che ____ tutti contenti del risultato.' (essere, loro)
explanation: The 3rd-person plural of essere's subjunctive is siano. The indicative sono (which also serves as the io form) is the slip. The archaic sieno is no longer standard.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative sono in must_not_include; archaic sieno also rejected.

prompt: Completa: 'Non credo che lui ____ ragione.' (avere)
explanation: Avere's subjunctive is abbia in all three singular persons (and abbiamo, abbiate, abbiano), with a double b and no h. Like sia, abbia is a compound auxiliary, so it is worth knowing cold. The indicative ha is the slip.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative ha in must_not_include; abia (single b) is the spelling slip.

prompt: Completa: 'Voglio che tu ____ un po' di pazienza.' (avere)
explanation: The tu subjunctive of avere is abbia, identical to the io and lui/lei forms. Two slips lurk here: the indicative hai, and the imperative abbi (abbi pazienza!), which is a different mood.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Note the imperative abbi is a plausible wrong form for tu; it is in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'È strano che loro non ____ ancora fame.' (avere)
explanation: The 3rd-person plural subjunctive of avere is abbiano (double b, no h). The indicative hanno is the slip; è strano che is an emotion/evaluation trigger that requires the subjunctive.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative 3pl hanno in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Spero che Maria ____ la risposta giusta.' (sapere)
explanation: Sapere's subjunctive is sappia, with a double p that the indicative (sa, sai, sa) never shows. The imperative sappi is a different mood; sapia with one p is a spelling slip.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative sa in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Bisogna che lei ti ____ una mano.' (dare)
explanation: Dare's subjunctive singular is dia (and diamo, diate, diano). The indicative dà, with its accent, is the slip; bisogna che requires the subjunctive.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative dà (and bare da) in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Mi auguro che tuo padre ____ meglio adesso.' (stare)
explanation: Stare's subjunctive singular is stia (and stiamo, stiate, stiano), patterning like dare's dia. The indicative sta is the slip; mi auguro che is a wish trigger that requires the subjunctive.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative sta in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Penso che i bambini ____ bene in quella scuola.' (stare)
explanation: The 3rd-person plural of stare's subjunctive is stiano. The indicative stanno is the slip to avoid.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative 3pl stanno in must_not_include.
